prose:
A Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on furan using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier `standard`) in the gas phase. The calculation used a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1 for the neutral molecule, and was performed on the geometry provided in `furan.xyz`. The Fukui indices, which describe the site-reactivity towards nucleophilic (f+), electrophilic (f-), and radical (f0) attack, were computed from single-point energy calculations on the neutral, cation (charge=1, mult=2), and anion (charge=-1, mult=2) species.

The analysis identifies atom C5 (the carbon atom at index 4) as the most electrophilic site, meaning it is the most susceptible to **nucleophilic attack**. It has the largest f+ index of **1.9242931**. The same atom is also the most nucleophilic site, most susceptible to **electrophilic attack**, with the largest f- index of 0.1616371. The results were computed with PySCF. The calculation converged successfully and reported no warnings.

***

**Fukui Reactivity Indices per Atom**

| Atom | Symbol | f+ (Nucleophilic Attack) | f- (Electrophilic Attack) | f0 (Radical Attack) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | O | -1.33162 | 0.08382 | -0.62390 |
| 2 | C | 1.22869 | 0.08910 | 0.65889 |
| 3 | C | 1.22703 | 0.08930 | 0.65817 |
| 4 | C | 1.92331 | 0.16153 | 1.04242 |
| 5 | C | 1.92429 | 0.16164 | 1.04297 |
| 6 | H | -1.21906 | 0.10248 | -0.55829 |
| 7 | H | -1.21911 | 0.10251 | -0.55830 |
| 8 | H | -0.76678 | 0.10482 | -0.33098 |
| 9 | H | -0.76674 | 0.10479 | -0.33097 |

***
